# Harden fleet closed-set token resolvers against prototype-shaped keys (hasOwn)

## Problem

The fleet cockpit primitive token resolvers use the `MAP[key] || fallback` idiom, which is **not a closed set**: a prototype-shaped key (`toString`, `constructor`, `hasOwnProperty`, `__proto__`) resolves to the inherited `Object.prototype` member (truthy) instead of the intended fallback, leaking a broken value (a function) into the rendered `--fm-*` custom property.

@neo-fable reproduced this on `familyToken` in the #14722 pre-review; it is **systemic** across the sibling resolvers:

- `kindToken` / `kindLabel` ŌĆö `apps/agentos/view/fleet/kindRegistry.mjs` (from #14701)
- `stateLabel` ŌĆö `apps/agentos/view/fleet/HealthSwatch.mjs` (from #14637)
- `stateToken` ŌĆö `apps/agentos/view/fleet/StateDot.mjs` (**merged**, #14593)

`familyToken` (#14722, `FamilyRail.mjs`) is already fixed ŌĆö it delegates to the hasOwn-guarded `isKnownFamily`.

## The fix

Each resolver: `return Object.hasOwn(MAP, key) ? MAP[key] : fallback` (or delegate to a hasOwn-guarded `isKnown*` predicate), so the closed set stays closed by construction.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `kindToken`, `kindLabel`, `stateLabel`, `stateToken` guard the map lookup with `Object.hasOwn` (no `MAP[k] ||` map-membership).
- [ ] Regression assertions per resolver: `toString` / `constructor` / `__proto__` resolve to the neutral/fallback (token) or literal (label), never an inherited member.
- [ ] One PR after #14701 / #14637 merge (all three files then live on dev) ŌĆö a single coherent closed-set-hardening change.

## Out of scope

`familyToken` (#14722, already hardened) ┬Ę any new resolver (the pattern is the guard, not the map).

## Notes

Low real-world likelihood (the maps are fed by the DTO's known vocabulary, not arbitrary input) ŌĆö this is correctness-by-construction robustness, not a live exploit. Durable lesson: a `MAP[key] || fallback` lookup is not a closed set.

- 2026-07-04T10:47:06Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `05344da` - "fix(agentos): kindRegistry resolvers guard the closed set against prototype-shaped keys (#14728)

kindToken and kindLabel used `MAP[k] || fallback`, which returns an inherited Object.prototype member for a prototype-shaped key ŌĆö so a kind of 'toString' / 'constructor' / '__proto__' leaked a function into the --fm-chip binding and the chip label instead of degrading to the neutral token / literal string.

Both resolvers now use an `Object.hasOwn` closed-set check ŌĆö the same guard familyToken, stateToken, and stateLabel carry. Prototype-key regression added to both resolver tests. This closes the last instance of the systemic pattern across the fleet resolver layer (familyToken done in the FamilyRail branch; stateToken/stateLabel in the HealthSwatch branch)."
